title: Worldwide lounge coverage in Cloudflare D1

sourceFamilies.label: Collinson access networks
sourceFamilies.benchmark: Priority Pass and LoungeKey are overlapping Collinson networks and must be deduped rather than counted independently.
sourceFamilies.acquisition: Approved workbook or bounded official public pages only.

sourceFamilies.label: Bank issuer lounge programs
sourceFamilies.benchmark: Issuer programs are access-policy evidence and sometimes operator evidence; never treat them as the only canonical location source.
sourceFamilies.acquisition: Official pages first; partner APIs or manual review for eligibility and guest policy.

sourceFamilies.label: Card network and benefit-platform programs
sourceFamilies.benchmark: Mastercard, Visa, and DragonPass surfaces overlap with Collinson and operator networks but add eligibility and regional coverage.
sourceFamilies.acquisition: Official public program pages and manual review. Do not use licensed or commercial inventory feeds.

sourceFamilies.label: Airline alliance lounge finders
sourceFamilies.benchmark: Alliance finders provide broad airline-operated and contract lounge coverage across Star Alliance, oneworld, and SkyTeam.
sourceFamilies.acquisition: Official finder pages; use as cross-check evidence for access, not automatic approval.

sourceFamilies.label: Airline-operated lounges
sourceFamilies.benchmark: Airline pages are the preferred source for operator-owned clubs, hours, restrictions, and temporary closures.
sourceFamilies.acquisition: Bounded official page intake through Playwright plus manual review for conflict resolution.

sourceFamilies.label: Independent and contract lounge operators
sourceFamilies.benchmark: Operator networks cover lounges missed by card/bank pages and must include Plaza Premium, Aspire, The Club, Escape, Marhaba, Primeclass, No1, and Be Relax.
sourceFamilies.acquisition: Official operator pages first. If public pages block automated intake, keep the lane in review instead of using licensed feeds.

sourceFamilies.label: Open normalization and enrichment
sourceFamilies.benchmark: Open data normalizes airports and enriches coordinates; it is not authoritative for lounge existence.
sourceFamilies.acquisition: OurAirports CSV, OSM/Taginfo monitoring, and Nominatim only as cached, rate-limited fallback.

validation.remoteSmokeCommand: npx wrangler d1 execute lounge-guru-catalog --remote --command="SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' ORDER BY name"

guardrails: Every lounge record must carry source provenance.
Licensed/private/commercial global lounge providers are outside the terminal goal.
Official public crawling must use Playwright, remain bounded, robots-aware, and snapshot-based.
Candidate records stay review-only until source conflicts, airport identity, hours, access policy, and location fields are resolved.
Cloudflare D1 is the canonical publish target for approved worldwide coverage.

benchmark.reason: Use a 2,500 physical-record approved minimum for the official-public Playwright lane because licensed/commercial global lounge inventory is excluded and overlapping source rows are deduped. Terminal success still requires dedupe across Priority Pass, public issuer evidence, alliances, airlines, operator-owned lounges, and open airport normalization.
